title: Module 1
content:
Properties of wood, strength of wood, Dugouts and Early Planked Boats,
Lashed/Sewn Boats in the Mediterranean Basin, Khufu’s Barge and Lisht
Timbers, planking, erections and joiner work, preservation.

title: Module 2
content:
Aluminium and its alloys, material characteristics of marine grade
aluminium, structural design considerations, welding and fabrication, TIG,
MIG, riveting, joining aluminium and steel, residual stresses and distortion,
fatigue and fracture design and analysis procedures, fire protection,
vibration, maintenance and repair.

title: Module 3
content:
Classification of polymers, types of polymerization, preparation,  properties
and uses of some important polymers,  fabrication of plastics, rubber,
synthetic rubbers, composites, difference between thermoplastics and
thermosets, types of resins, glass and carbon fibres, different types of
fabrics and mats such as chopped stranded mats, woven roving  and their
properties, FRP, GRP materials, different types of moulding techniques,
lay-up
techniques,
manufacturing
requirements,
advantages
and
disadvantages of composites over steel and aluminium in shipbuilding.

title: Module 4
content:
Marine concrete, history of concrete ships, prestressed concrete vessels,
advantages and disadvantages, future trends in concrete ships, advanced
materials, use of adhesive bonding on superstructures, laser-welded steel
sandwich panels.
